methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $500M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Kairos Power is a late-stage nuclear energy company founded by Mike Laufer, Edward Blandford, and Per Peterson. The company has raised over $500M to date. The nuclear energy sector includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 5 private peers (Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, TerraPower), though a sufficiently tight peer set at the same stage and valuation tier could not be established.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology was the primary applicable approach. Using the $500M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for late-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $600M. This method reflects typical venture progression patterns but relies on raised capital as a proxy rather than operating metrics or comparable transaction multiples.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $510M to $690M, with a median of $600M. Key limitations include the absence of sufficient same-stage nuclear peers with disclosed valuations, which prevented conventional peer-median triangulation. The valuation is therefore sensitive to refinement in comparable transaction data within the nuclear energy sector and to any updates on Kairos Power's operating milestones, capital deployment rates, or path to revenue. Material uncertainty remains regarding appropriate valuation multiples for pre-revenue nuclear infrastructure developers.
